one_time_description: In December 2020 (subsequent event), the First Nation settled a land-based claim with the Government of Canada and received $47,684,156 in settlement compensation.
economic_notes: First Nation receives 73% of revenue from ISC. Under construction: two duplexes, administration building, and water treatment plant (total contracts ~$28.2M). Flood claim and Highway 71 claim negotiations ongoing with Canada and Ontario. New Gold Inc. equity investment held at cost ($361,125). Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership distributes gaming revenues to First Nation.
notes: Fiscal year is 2020 (March 31, 2020), not 2024. Auditor is MNP LLP (Kenora). Prior year (2019) was audited by BDO Canada LLP. CMHC Housing is consolidated as a subsidiary; Ontario First Nations Limited Partnership is accounted for outside consolidation (nominal carrying value, equity method not applied - treated as GBE). OSR is negative because deferred revenue current-year drawdowns reduce net recognized revenue significantly below government transfers. Large subsequent event: $47.68M land claim settlement received December 2020. Major capital projects underway totalling ~$28.2M in contracts. COVID-19 noted as emerging risk. First Nation is in Ontario, not Alberta.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (114.68%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
